Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Ph21511 is explicitly the contig identified and later designated PhpCAT in the Results.

Source_Species.reason: The gene is from petunia; the paper consistently studies Petunia hybrida cv Mitchell flowers and names the transporter P. hybrida plastidial cationic amino-acid transporter.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Petunia hybrida is explicitly stated throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify P. hybrida cv. Mitchell diploid plants (W-115).

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses in planta were performed in petunia flowers.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Transgenic and metabolic analyses were done in Petunia hybrida cv. Mitchell diploid.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods explicitly state transformation and growth of P. hybrida cv. Mitchell diploid (W-115).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Ph21511 was annotated as a putative cationic amino-acid transporter (CAT; T.C. 2.A.3.3.4), a member of the amino acid–polyamine–organocation transporter family, with 14 transmembrane helices; TargetP predicted the first 43 aa as a plastid-targeting signal.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GFP fusion and plastid immunoblotting confirmed localization to plastids in Results Fig. 2.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qRT-PCR showed expression in all flower organs and leaves, lowest at bud stage, increased after flower opening, and rhythmic with highest transcript abundance at night on day 2 postanthesis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This restates the petunia expression pattern shown in Fig. 3, including induction during flower opening and nighttime peak on day 2 in corollas.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state both RNAi and overexpression constructs were driven by the Clarkia breweri linalool synthase (LIS) petal-specific promoter.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The principal measured end products affected by PhpCAT manipulation were phenylalanine-derived volatiles.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper refers to 'phenylalanine-derived volatiles' and elsewhere mentions phenylpropanoids and benzenoids generally, but it does not explicitly classify the measured terminal metabolites as the exact class phrase 'Phenylpropanoid/benzenoid specialized metabolites' for PhpCAT.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper discusses floral volatiles/scent and reproduction in general background, but does not directly demonstrate or explicitly state pollinator-attraction function for the PhpCAT-affected metabolites in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Volatiles were measured as emitted from detached flowers, and RNA/metabolite work focused on corollas/petals.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support that PhpCAT exports aromatic amino acids, especially linked in planta to plastidial phenylalanine export; RNAi decreased and overexpression increased phenylalanine-derived volatiles and phenylalanine/tyrosine pools, with flux effects on the plastidial pathway.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described and used: coexpression analysis, GFP targeting, plastid immunoblot, E. coli radiolabel assays, RNAi, overexpression, metabolite profiling, and 15N-tyrosine flux analysis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results/Methods explicitly involve daily light/dark rhythmic analysis, developmental stage comparisons, and feeding with 10 mM 15N-tyrosine for flux analysis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: These outcomes match Fig. 5a: RNAi reduced volatile emission by 20–42%, lowered phenylalanine, tyrosine, prephenate, and arogenate, with only up to 9% reduction in shikimate and 5–18% decrease in tryptophan.

Omics_Data.reason: Methods describe RNA-seq of petunia corollas with six libraries on Illumina HiSeq2500 and de novo contigs including Ph21511; no formal accession number is provided, matching 'accession number NA'.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application statement inferable from the findings and Discussion, but it is not directly tested or explicitly framed as breeding/engineering value in the Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show PhpCAT transports phenylalanine, tyrosine, and tryptophan in radiolabel assays, while in planta/flux data specifically support a key role in plastidial phenylalanine export and altered flux partitioning; feedback inhibition is presented as a likely mechanism.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered statement accurately summarizes explicit Results: identification by homology/coexpression, plastid localization and aromatic amino-acid transport, RNAi decreases, overexpression increases, and flux shifts in knockdowns.
